Quant ELSS Tax Saver Fund Direct-Growth Fund Key Highlights 1. Current NAV: The Current Net Asset Value of the Quant ELSS Tax Saver Fund - Direct Plan as of Jun 25, 2026 is Rs 456.48 for Growth option of its Direct plan. 2. Returns: Its trailing returns over different time periods are: 14.96% (1yr), 18.71% (3yr), 17.69% (5yr) and 20.0% (since launch). Whereas, Category returns for the same time duration are: 1.11% (1yr), 13.09% (3yr) and 12.34% (5yr). 3. Fund Size: The Quant ELSS Tax Saver Fund - Direct Plan currently holds Assets under Management worth of Rs 13070.35 crore as on May 31, 2026. 4. Expense ratio: The expense ratio of the fund is 1.78% for Direct plan as on Jun 19, 2026. 5. Exit Load: The given fund doesn't attract any Exit Load. 6. Minimum Investment: Minimum investment
Exit Load: The given fund doesn't attract any Exit Load. 6. Minimum Investment: Minimum investment required is Rs 500 and minimum additional investment is Rs 500. Minimum SIP investment is Rs 500.

Beta

Beta

Beta shows the portfolio risk in relation to the market. A beta of less than 1 means that the fund returns are less volatile compared to the broader market. A beta of more than 1 means that the fund returns are more volatile than the broader markets. A beta equal to 1 means that fund's volatility is in line with the broader market.

About Quant ELSS Tax Saver Fund Direct-Growth 1. Quant ELSS Tax Saver Fund - Direct Plan is Open-ended ELSS Equity scheme which belongs to Quant Mutual Fund House. 2. The fund was launched on Jan 01, 2013. Investment objective & Benchmark 1. The investment objective of the fund is that " The scheme aims to generate capital appreciation by investing predominantly in equity shares with growth potential. The secondary objective is to give dividend and other income. " 2. It is benchmarked against NIFTY 500 Total Return Index. Asset Allocation & Portfolio Composition 1. The asset allocation of the fund comprises around 97.05% in equities, 0.0% in debts and 2.95% in cash & cash equivalents. 2. While the top 10 equity holdings constitute around 69.12% of the assets, the top 3 sectors constitute

the top 10 equity holdings constitute around 69.12% of the assets, the top 3 sectors constitute around 50.16% of the assets. 3. The fund largely follows a Growth oriented style of investing and invests across market capitalisations - around 0.0% in giant & large cap companies, 0.0% in mid cap and 0.0% in small cap companies. Tax Implications on Quant ELSS Tax Saver Fund Direct-Growth 1. Short-term Capital Gain Tax - STCG is not applicable on ELSS schemes since these have a lock-in period of 3 years. 2. Investment into ELSS schemes qualify for tax deduction under Section 80C of the Indian Income Tax Act. 3. Gains of more than Rs. 1 lakh after completion of 3 years will be taxed at a rate of 10% (Long-term Capital Gain Tax - LTCG). 4. For Dividend Distribution Tax, the dividend income from

10% (Long-term Capital Gain Tax - LTCG). 4. For Dividend Distribution Tax, the dividend income from this fund will get added to the income of an investor and taxed according to his/her respective tax slabs. 5. Also, for dividend income in excess of Rs 5,000 in a financial year; the fund house shall deduct a TDS of 10% on such income.

FAQs about Quant ELSS Tax Saver Fund Direct-Growth

Is it safe to invest in Quant ELSS Tax Saver Fund - Direct Plan?

As per SEBI’s latest guidelines to calculate risk grades, investment in the Quant ELSS Tax Saver Fund - Direct Plan comes under Very High risk category.

What is the category of Quant ELSS Tax Saver Fund - Direct Plan?

Quant ELSS Tax Saver Fund - Direct Plan belongs to the Equity : ELSS category of funds.

How Long should I Invest in Quant ELSS Tax Saver Fund - Direct Plan?

How Long should I Invest in Quant ELSS Tax Saver Fund - Direct Plan?

The suggested investment horizon of investing into Quant ELSS Tax Saver Fund - Direct Plan is >3 years. The suggested investment horizon is the minimum time required for holding investments in the fund to reduce its downside risk and ensure that the returns become more predictable.

Who manages the Quant ELSS Tax Saver Fund - Direct Plan?

The Quant ELSS Tax Saver Fund - Direct Plan is managed by Archit Singhal (Since Jan 01, 2013) , Sanjeev Sharma (Since Feb 03, 2025) , Ankit A Pande (Since May 11, 2020) , Sandeep Tandon (Since Feb 03, 2025) , Varun Pattani (Since Feb 03, 2025) and Ayusha Kumbhat (Since Feb 19, 2025).

India's Economic Indicators

| 26 June, 2026, 04:59 PM IST |

Today's ePaper

Business NewsNewsEconomy India's Economic Indicators

Economy Dashboard

This Economic Dashboard provides a comprehensive collection of India's economic indicators, offering users access to current and historical data across various sectors. The platform covers key metrics such as GDP growth rates, inflation rates, GST, infrastructure output, services PMI, manufacturing PMI, and fiscal and trade deficits. Each indicator is accompanied by its latest value, previous figures, and historical highs and lows, enabling users to analyse trends over time. This resource is a valuable tool for economists, analysts and individuals seeking detailed insights into India's economic performance.

INDIA'S ECONOMIC INDICATORS

Economy Dashboard

The ET’s Economic Dashboard page provides a detailed and data-driven overview of key economic indicators in India. It serves as a valuable resource for economists, analysts, investors, policymakers, and researchers who seek up-to-date information on India's economic performance. The page is structured to display economic indicators across multiple categories, including GDP growth, inflation, fiscal deficit numbers, trade data, GST, core sector output, services and manufacturing sector PMI. Overview of Economic Indicators The dashboard presents real-time and historical data on India's macroeconomic indicators. Users can access statistics on GDP growth rates, inflation trends and WPI. Additionally, fiscal numbers, trade deficit, GST, core sector output, services and manufacturing sector PMI

fiscal numbers, trade deficit, GST, core sector output, services and manufacturing sector PMI are available. Each indicator is presented with its latest recorded value, previous readings, historical highs and lows, and graphical representations of trends over time. This structured approach helps users analyse patterns and assess the direction of India's economy. The data is updated regularly, making it a reliable source for tracking economic movements. Key Sections of the Page Gross Domestic Product (GDP): The GDP section covers India's overall economic output, breaking it down into growth rates, sector-wise contributions, and quarterly and annual data. This helps users understand the country's economic expansion and contraction trends. Infra output This section showcases key data on

economic expansion and contraction trends. Infra output This section showcases key data on India's infrastructure output -- a (monthly) performance gauge of the industries that come under the infra bracket. India's infra output is a combined index that measures the performance of 8 Core Industries: refinery (weight: 28%), electricity generation (19.9%), steel (17.9%), coal (10.3%), crude oil (8.9%), natural gas (6.9%), cement (5.4%) and fertilizers (2.6%). The infra sector make up around 40 per cent of India's total industrial output. Trade deficit India has historically run a trade deficit because it imports more goods and services than it exports. As per govt data, India's sustained trade deficit is because of the strong imports growth in particularly mineral fuels, oils and waxes and

deficit is because of the strong imports growth in particularly mineral fuels, oils and waxes and bituminous substances and pearls, precious and semi-precious stones and jewellery. In recent years, India has had the biggest deficits with China, Switzerland, Saudi Arabia, Iraq and Indonesia. GST GST collection is one of the primary streams of revenue for the govt. This data set is keenly watched as a gauge of the health of govt finances. The Finance Ministry began releasing monthly GST revenue collection data via official press release (Press Information Bureau) from May 2018. The issuance of state-wise monthly collection data began from January 2020. Imports This section provides data on India's import basket. India's top import partners include China, the US, UAE, Saudi Arabia and

on India's import basket. India's top import partners include China, the US, UAE, Saudi Arabia and Switzerland. The top items imported by India include mineral fuels, oils and waxes and bituminous substances; pearls, precious and semi-precious stones and jewellery; electrical machinery and equipment; nuclear reactors, boilers, machinery and mechanical appliances; and organic chemicals. Exports India's export basket is spread across 200 countries/territories and 31 commodities. The country's total export value between in FY25 was $433.56 billion. India's biggest export destinations (early 2025 data) include US, UAE, Netherlands, China and Singapore. The US has consistently been the top market for Indian exporters. India top export items include refined petroleum, mineral fuels, machinery

for Indian exporters. India top export items include refined petroleum, mineral fuels, machinery and mechanical appliances, electrical machinery, organic chemicals, vehicles, pharmaceutical products, medical appliances, iron and steel, cereals, apparel, cotton, seafood, sugar and various other agri products. Fiscal deficit Fiscal deficit is the difference between the government's total expenditure and its total revenue (excluding borrowed funds) in a certain fiscal year. It shows how much the government would need to borrow to finance its work when its income falls short of its expenses. It is often expressed as a percentage of the country's Gross Domestic Product (GDP). India's government recorded a fiscal deficit to 4.8% of GDP in FY2024–25, down from 5.9% in the previous fiscal year.

recorded a fiscal deficit to 4.8% of GDP in FY2024–25, down from 5.9% in the previous fiscal year. Government Budget in India averaged -5.15 percent of GDP from 1970 until 2024, reaching an all time high of -2.53 percent of GDP in 1973 and a record low of -9.16 percent of GDP in 2020. Retail (CPI) inflation Retail inflation is one of the most keenly watched economic data points in India. It is also called Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation. It represents the change in retail prices of goods & services, which impacts household budgets. The CPI number is based on a fixed basket of goods and services, the composition of which the govt might, or might not, change from time to time. In 2013, the CPI replaced the wholesale price index (WPI) as a main measure of inflation. The most important

the CPI replaced the wholesale price index (WPI) as a main measure of inflation. The most important category in India's CPI is Food and beverages (45.86% of total weight), of which cereals and products (9.67%), Milk and products (6.61%), Vegetables (6.04%), Prepared meals, snacks, sweets, etc. (5.55%), Meat and fish (3.61%), and Oils and fats (3.56%). Miscellaneous accounts for 28.32%, of which Transport and communication (8.59%), health (5.89%), and education (4.46%). Housing accounts for 10.07%; Fuel and light for 6.84%; Clothing and footwear for 6.53%; and Pan, tobacco and intoxicants for 2.38% (official data). CPI in India averaged 141.47 points from 2011 until 2025, touching an all-time high of 197.00 points in August of 2025 and a record low of 86.81 points in February of 2011.

high of 197.00 points in August of 2025 and a record low of 86.81 points in February of 2011. Wholesale Price Index (WPI) The WPI tracks the price of a representative basket of wholesale items. In India, wholesale price index is divided into three groups: Primary Articles (22.6% of total weight); Fuel and Power (13.2%); and Manufactured Products (64.2%). The Food Index from the Primary Articles and Manufactured Products groups accounts for 24.4% of the total weight. The most important components of the Manufactured Products group are Basic Metals (9.7% of total weight); Food products (9.1%); Chemicals and Chemical products (6.5%) and Textiles (4.9%). Within the Primary Articles group, the most important component is Food Articles (15.3%), while within Fuel and Power, the most important

most important component is Food Articles (15.3%), while within Fuel and Power, the most important category is HSD (3.1%). WPI in India averaged 108.62 points from 2004 until 2025, reaching an all time high of 156.70 points in October of 2024 and a record low of 62.44 points in April of 2004 (govt data). Manufacturing PMI The S&P Global India Manufacturing Purchasing Managers’ Index tracks the performance of the country's manufacturing sector. It is prepared based on a survey of 500 manufacturing companies. The Manufacturing PMI is derived from 5 separate indexes so weighed: New Orders (30%), Output (25%), Employment (20%), Suppliers’ Delivery Times (15%) and Stock of Items Purchased (10%). The Delivery Times index is inverted to make it move in a comparable direction. A reading above 50

The Delivery Times index is inverted to make it move in a comparable direction. A reading above 50 in a certain month means an expansion over the previous month, while under 50 means a contraction. Services PMI The HSBC India Services PMI is compiled and published by S&P Global. The data is collected via monthly questionnaires answered by purchasing managers at about 350 service sector companies across the country. The index measures the movement of variables such as sales, employment, inventories and prices. A reading above 50 indicates an expansion, while under 50 suggests a decline. (The three-year, five-year and 10-year data points have been taken from Deloitte, and the one-year data points from official govt sources and the PMI data has been sourced from S&P Global.)

Eighth Pay Panel may stay cautious on fitment factor

Eighth Pay Panel may stay cautious on fitment factor

While the Commission hasn't yet finalised its recommendations, early discussions suggest that the fitment factor could remain close to the 2.57 multiplier adopted by the Seventh Pay Commission, even as employee unions continue to push for a higher revision.

RBI remains watchful of global, monsoon situation says Sanjay Malhotra

RBI remains watchful of global, monsoon situation says Sanjay Malhotra

RBI remains watchful of global, monsoon situation says Sanjay Malhotra

The Reserve Bank of India is adopting a cautious "wait and watch" approach regarding global geopolitical shifts and monsoon progress. Governor Sanjay Malhotra stated that while inflation hasn't broadly impacted consumer prices, uncertainties persist. He highlighted that de-escalation in West Asia is positive, but risks remain. The RBI is monitoring crude prices and monsoon impact on growth, confident in India's preparedness for challenges.
